name: Verify Numerical Reasoning
description: Measures ability to interpret numerical data including graphs, charts, and tables. Ideal for finance, data analysis, engineering, and management roles requiring quantitative decision-making.

name: Verify Verbal Reasoning
description: Assesses ability to understand written passages and draw logical conclusions. Suitable for roles in HR, communications, law, management, and any position requiring strong reading comprehension.

name: Verify Inductive Reasoning
description: Evaluates abstract reasoning and ability to identify patterns in unfamiliar data. Used for graduate, technical, and analytical roles where problem-solving with new information is critical.

name: Verify Deductive Reasoning
description: Measures logical reasoning from given premises to conclusions. Relevant for roles in technology, law, consulting, and any domain requiring structured logical thinking.

name: Verify Checking
description: Tests speed and accuracy in checking data for errors, such as names, codes, and numbers. Best suited for administrative, clerical, data entry, and compliance-related positions.

name: Verify Mechanical Comprehension
description: Assesses understanding of mechanical and physical concepts. Appropriate for engineering, manufacturing, maintenance, and technical apprenticeship roles.

name: Verify Spatial Reasoning
description: Measures ability to mentally manipulate shapes and visualize spatial relationships. Useful for roles in architecture, engineering design, surgery, and technical drafting.

name: Verify G+ Adaptive Ability
description: Adaptive cognitive ability test measuring general mental ability (g-factor) across verbal, numerical, and inductive dimensions. Suitable for all professional and graduate-level hiring.

name: Verify Interactive Numerical Reasoning
description: An interactive, scenario-based version of numerical reasoning. Candidates solve realistic business problems using data. Ideal for analyst, finance, and strategy roles.

name: Verify Interactive Verbal Reasoning
description: An interactive, scenario-based version of verbal reasoning placed in a realistic work context. Suitable for management, consulting, and communication-heavy roles.

name: OPQ32r
description: The Occupational Personality Questionnaire (OPQ32r) measures 32 personality characteristics relevant to workplace behavior. Widely used for leadership assessment, team fit, and competency profiling across all levels.

name: Motivation Questionnaire (MQ)
description: Assesses 18 factors that increase or decrease an individual's motivation at work. Used to evaluate cultural fit, role engagement, and retention risk during hiring or development.

name: Customer Contact Styles Questionnaire (CCSQ)
description: Measures personality traits critical for customer-facing roles such as empathy, patience, and service orientation. Ideal for customer service, retail, banking, and support positions.

name: Sales Potential Questionnaire (SPQ)
description: Personality questionnaire designed specifically to identify traits associated with high sales performance, including drive, persuasiveness, and resilience. Used for sales representative and account manager roles.

name: Service and Values Questionnaire (SVQ)
description: Measures alignment with organizational values and service attitude. Used for frontline, hospitality, and customer care hiring where cultural and values fit is critical.

name: Customer Focus SJT
description: Situational Judgment Test presenting realistic customer service scenarios. Candidates choose the best responses. Ideal for call center agents, retail staff, and hospitality workers.

name: Teamwork SJT
description: Evaluates judgment in collaborative work scenarios, including handling conflict, sharing credit, and supporting colleagues. Suitable for any team-based role.

name: Entry Level Sales SJT
description: Situational judgment scenarios for early-career sales roles. Assesses prospecting, objection handling, and closing judgment. Good for SDR, BDR, and inside sales positions.

name: Supervisory Practices SJT
description: Presents realistic supervisory dilemmas to assess management judgment including delegation, performance management, and team motivation. Designed for team leaders and first-line managers.

name: Java 8 (New)
description: Assesses practical knowledge of Java 8 features including streams, lambdas, functional interfaces, the Optional class, and concurrency utilities. Suitable for mid-to-senior Java developers.

name: Core Java
description: Tests foundational Java knowledge including OOP concepts, data structures, exception handling, and the Collections framework. Suitable for entry-level and junior Java developers.

name: Python (New)
description: Evaluates Python programming skills including syntax, data structures, list comprehensions, decorators, file I/O, and standard libraries. Applicable for backend, data science, and automation roles.

name: JavaScript (New)
description: Measures knowledge of modern JavaScript (ES6+) including closures, promises, async/await, DOM manipulation, and common design patterns. For frontend and full-stack developer roles.

name: SQL (New)
description: Tests ability to write and optimize SQL queries including JOINs, subqueries, window functions, and indexing. Relevant for data analysts, database administrators, and backend developers.

name: C++ (New)
description: Assesses C++ programming knowledge including memory management, pointers, object-oriented design, templates, and the STL. Ideal for systems programmers, game developers, and embedded engineers.

name: .NET Framework
description: Evaluates knowledge of the Microsoft .NET ecosystem including C#, ASP.NET, LINQ, and Entity Framework. Suitable for enterprise application developers in Microsoft technology stacks.

name: R Programming
description: Tests proficiency in R for statistical computing, data wrangling with tidyverse, and visualization with ggplot2. Used for data scientist and statistical analyst hiring.

name: Machine Learning (New)
description: Assesses knowledge of machine learning algorithms, model evaluation, feature engineering, and frameworks like scikit-learn. For data scientist, ML engineer, and AI researcher roles.

name: Data Science
description: Evaluates end-to-end data science competency including EDA, statistical modeling, machine learning, and data storytelling. Ideal for data scientist and analytics engineering roles.

name: Cloud Computing (AWS)
description: Tests knowledge of AWS core services including EC2, S3, Lambda, IAM, and VPC. Suitable for cloud engineers, DevOps professionals, and solutions architects.

name: Cybersecurity Fundamentals
description: Assesses understanding of network security, encryption, threat models, and secure coding practices. Appropriate for security analyst, SOC engineer, and IT security roles.

name: Agile Software Development
description: Measures understanding of Agile methodologies including Scrum, Kanban, sprint planning, and retrospectives. Useful for developers, scrum masters, and product owners in agile organizations.

name: Microsoft Excel (Advanced)
description: Tests advanced Excel skills including pivot tables, VLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH, macros, and data modeling. Relevant for financial analysts, operations managers, and business intelligence roles.

name: Android Development
description: Assesses knowledge of Android SDK, activity lifecycle, Kotlin/Java for Android, and UI design patterns. For mobile developer roles targeting the Android platform.

name: iOS Development
description: Evaluates Swift, UIKit, SwiftUI, and iOS app lifecycle knowledge. Suitable for mobile developers building applications for Apple platforms.

name: Graduate 8.0
description: A multi-measure cognitive battery combining verbal, numerical, and inductive reasoning. Calibrated specifically for graduate and entry-level professional recruitment benchmarks.
